examples.text: جَزَاؤُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّٰتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا
examples.english: Their reward is with their Lord - the gardens of Eden [with] rivers running beneath them - wherein they abide forever.

examples.text: وَكَانَ نَهْرٌ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ عَدْنٍ لِيَسْقِيَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ، وَمِنْ هُنَاكَ يَنْقَسِمُ فَيَصِيرُ أَرْبَعَةَ رُؤُوسٍ.
examples.english: And a river is going out from Eden to water the garden, and from there it is parted, and has become four chief [rivers];

senses.gloss: Aden (a port city, the largest city in Yemen; the former capital of South Yemen)
to stay, to bide, to remain
to strike sharply with a hoe or the like
to dung, to manure, to fertilize
to dung, to manure, to fertilize
to strike sharply (e.g. with the miner’s pickaxe صَاقُور (ṣāqūr)), to mine in

examples.text: وتعرف أيضا بشجرة عدنة وهي نبات معمر يتواجد في المناطق الجبلية وشبه الجبلية كجزيرة سوقطرى في اليمن ومدينة جازان في السعودية كما توجد كذلك في الهند والصين وبورما.
examples.english: It is also known as ʕadana-tree and it is a perennial plant found in mountainous and semi-mountainous regions like Socotra, in Yemen, in the city of Jazan in Saudi-Arabia, and also India, China and Burma.

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Biblical Hebrew עֵדֶן (ʿḗden)bor.
Arabic عدن
Borrowed from Biblical Hebrew עֵדֶן (ʿḗden).

Denominal verb of عِدَّان (ʕiddān, “set time; set place of staying”).

Adenium obesum
Probably a local distortion from (the plural of) عُود (ʕūd, “wood; agarwood, any plant with high moisture content”).
